agenticWorkspace.effectiveRuntimeCommand: python -c "import sys; from command_generation.console import main_for_entrypoint; raise SystemExit(main_for_entrypoint('agentic-workspace', sys.argv[1:]))"

promotion_requires: help output identifies supported generated commands and the weak-agent routing status
unsupported command errors give a compact recovery path instead of leaving agents to infer next steps
runtime handoff failures are classified as adapter runtime handoff failures with recovery guidance
black-box conformance covers normal read-only behavior, unsupported command recovery, and runtime handoff failure mapping
generated-package proof advertises the target as weak-agent-safe only after these checks pass
maturity.summary: Generated target is safe to route to weaker agents for supported read-only commands.

runtimeBinding.default_runtime_command: python -c "import sys; from command_generation.console import main_for_entrypoint; raise SystemExit(main_for_entrypoint('agentic-workspace', sys.argv[1:]))"
error_mapping: Generated adapter failures before process handoff are adapter failures.
Non-zero exits returned by the delegated canonical process are runtime failures unless the conformance case expects the error.
Generation freshness and no-direct-edit failures remain generation-layer failures before runtime execution starts.
runtime_owns: canonical JSON or text output behavior
runtimeBinding.scope: Read-only generated non-Python adapters may invoke the existing Python command surface as the runtime boundary; mutation-capable commands remain deferred until lifecycle dry-run, preflight, and refusal conformance are explicit.
runtimeBinding.selected_model: generated parser/help with process handoff to canonical Python CLI
